title: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : East District Council (markets) Bye-laws(south)
title_en: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : East District Council (markets) Bye-laws(south)

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Bye-laws may be cited as the South-East District Council (Markets) Bye-laws.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Establishment and control of markets
provisions.content:
3. Establishment and control of markets
(1) The Council may establish and designate areas within the jurisdiction of the Council as markets.
(2) Every market shall be under the control and supervision of a market master appointed by the Council.

provisions.title: Permit to operate stall in market

definitions.definition: a health inspector employed by the Government or by the Council
a licensing officer appointed by the Council for the purpose of these Bye-laws
a market established by the Council
a person appointed by the Council to be a market master for the purpose of these Bye-laws
any building, stand, shelter, table, place or plot within a market set aside for the sale of merchandise or for the provision of a service to the public
a person by whom a stall is hired under these Bye-laws.
